assertions.description: document.compatMode reports CSS1Compat (standards) or BackCompat (quirks); the change targets BackCompat documents.
getElementsByName is case-sensitive in all modes — used as a parallel surface that already matched what fragment navigation now does.
ID selectors are case-sensitive — the same rule fragment navigation now adopts in quirks mode.
After the change, location.hash='#Foo' on a quirks document does NOT scroll to <a name="foo"> — the id with different case must not be the indicated element.
assertions.test:
(async()=>{const f=document.createElement('iframe');f.style.cssText='position:absolute;left:-9999px;top:-9999px;width:320px;height:120px;border:0';document.body.appendChild(f);try{const d=f.contentDocument;d.open();d.write('quirks probe
target
');d.close();await new Promise(r=>setTimeout(r,50));f.contentWindow.location.hash='FragLowerOnly';await new Promise(r=>setTimeout(r,80));return d.compatMode==='BackCompat'&&f.contentWindow.scrollY<200;}finally{f.remove();}})()

assertions.description: Element.id round-trips with original case (the DOM has always stored ID with original case; matching is what changed).
